_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-14 14:37:31Z)**_

Run it from the `deck-generator/` directory:

```
cd deck-generator && npm run dev
```

That starts the Vite dev server. Other scripts: `npm run build`, `npm run preview`, `npm run export`.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-14 14:40:12Z)**_

The binary exists but isn't on your PATH. Use npx or the script directly:

```
npx vite
```

Or reinstall to refresh the npm script symlinks:

```
npm install
npm run dev
```

`npm run dev` should resolve `vite` from `node_modules/.bin` automatically — if it's not, your `node_modules` is likely partially installed. `npm install` will fix it.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-14 14:41:08Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-14 14:41:11Z)**_

Found it. The parent folder `Conferences:Webinars` contains a colon, which is the PATH separator on macOS/Linux. When npm prepends `node_modules/.bin` to PATH, the colon splits the path and the shell can't find vite.

Two fixes:

**Option 1 — run vite directly (quick):**
```
node node_modules/vite/bin/vite.js
```

**Option 2 — rename the folder (proper fix):**
```
mv "/Users/stugrey/Documents/Student Voice ICD/Conferences:Webinars" "/Users/stugrey/Documents/Student Voice ICD/Conferences-Webinars"
```

Then `npm run dev` will work normally. I'd recommend Option 2 — the colon will keep breaking other tools too (not just npm).

---
